channel.title: ToA
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: Session 2
item.description:
The adventurers had previously secured essential gear, such as insect repellent balm and rain catchers. Sir Caldryn Keyne spent time at the Thundering Lizard inn attempting to gather rumors from locals about the city's princes and the island. This led to an encounter with two guards who were looking for him.
The guards informed Sir Caldryn Keyne that his attempts to recruit companions for his entourage were unauthorized, noting that Merchant Prince Jobal handles guides and sellswords in the city. Sir Caldryn Keyne defused the situation by claiming he was merely seeking a young intern for tutelage, and he was advised to seek an audience with Jobal to follow proper protocol.
The party settled in for their long rest. Scarloc spent his time doing aerobic exercises and Mistral reading on the roof of the inn.
The next morning, preparations focused on the journey ahead. The party confirmed they would be traveling with the guide they met previously, who offered her services for free in exchange for help defeating Nanny Pu'pu in the town of Mbala.
During breakfast, the Druid, Mistral, used their daily ability to produce Goodberry to help provision the group for the journey. Tidus performed a ritual of prayer, giving thanks to the water goddess.After meeting their guide, the party decided to travel at a fast pace to make rapid progress, driven by the urgency of the death curse. A Druidcraft cantrip confirmed the weather would be a constant drizzle for the entire day.
With the aid of Tidus Thalorien's Guidance cantrip and the guide (Eku) the navigator (Scarloc) successfully charted a course, allowing the party to cover two hexes of the jungle during their first day of travel. The terrain was difficult, described as messy and mushy, though the guide suggested a route that bypassed areas of known undead activity.
As night fell, the party set up camp. Mistral Honeygleam, Tidus Thalorien, and Scarloc Farmguard worked together to secure the camp, setting up their tents and preparing food using the supplies, supplemented by the magical berries. During this time, Sir Caldryn Keyne continued his efforts at "tutelage" of Scarloc, sharing lessons on being a "chivalent hero" and serving wine from his private reserve.The long rest was violently interrupted when the camp was ambushed by large, raptor-like dinosaurs.
In the ensuing combat, Sir Caldryn Keyne was immediately incapacitated by a powerful strike. His life was saved by Mistral's flying monkey familiar, which provided him with a Goodberry, returning him to 1 HP. The rest of the party engaged the monsters. Scarloc Farmguard landed decisive blows. The party ultimately defeated the three dinosaurs.

item.title: Scarloc Farmguard
item.description:
While staying at the inn in Port Nyanzaru, Scarloc spent his time performing aerobic exercises during the long rest.
When the party began their journey, Scarloc was designated the navigator. With the aid of Tidus Thalorien's Guidance cantrip and the guide (Eku), Scarloc successfully charted a course, allowing the party to cover two hexes on their first day of travel.
While setting up camp, Scarloc helped Mistral and Tidus secure the area and prepare food. He also received lessons on being a "chivalent hero" from Sir Caldryn Keyne. During the ambush by raptor-like dinosaurs, Scarloc landed decisive blows against the monsters.

item.title: Nanny Pu'pu
item.description:
Nanny Pu'pu is an infamous figure dwelling in the jungle of Chult. She is the central figure of the first major quest undertaken by the adventurers.
She currently dwells in the town of Mbala.
The existence of Nanny Pu'pu became relevant to the party during initial inquiries in Port Nyanzaru while they were seeking guides and information about their expedition into Chult.
A woman named Eku approached the party at an inn and offered her services as a guide.
Eku revealed that she was seeking to eradicate an evil dwelling in Mbala, specifically naming Nanny Pu'pu.
Eku offered her services for free if the party agreed to help her destroy this evil.

item.title: Mbala
item.description:
Mbala is a significant location in the uncharted jungle of Chult, notable as the destination of the adventurers' first major expedition.
Mbala is the location of the first major objective undertaken by the party in the island of Chult.
The town of Mbala is the designated target for the party's current journey, initiated during their time in Port Nyanzaru.
A guide, a woman named Eku, approached the party seeking to hire them.
Eku revealed that she was seeking to eradicate the evil dwelling in the town of Mbala.
The specific source of this evil is known as Nanny Pu'pu. The guide offered her services for free if the party agreed to help her destroy this evil, an arrangement the adventurers accepted.
The party agreed to help defeat Nanny Pu'pu in the town of Mbala and are currently traveling toward this destination.

item.title: Eku
item.description:
Eku is a guide encountered in Port Nyanzaru who offered her services to the adventurers in exchange for assistance on her own quest.
Eku approached the party while they were at an inn in Port Nyanzaru, seeking to be hired.
Eku revealed she was embarking on a personal quest and inquired if the party was heading toward Mbala.
Her mission is to eradicate the evil dwelling in the town of Mbala, specifically targeting Nanny Pu'pu. Eku offered her guiding services for free if the party agreed to help her destroy this evil. The party accepted her offer and are currently traveling with her. Eku confirmed that she would handle guiding and navigation for the expedition, although the party remained responsible for their food and water.

item.title: Session 1
item.description:
The first session of the Tomb of Annihilation module began with the presentation of the worldwide "death curse". This affliction targets everyone who has ever been raised from the dead, causing them to wither and die, and preventing future resurrections.The player characters—including Mistral Honeygleam (a cheerful, small, level 1 caster), and Sir Caldryn Keyne (an arrogant, well-dressed, wealthy High Elf/Sun Elf paladin)—were gathered at a house in Baldur's Gate. They were acquainted and had worked together previously.
The party's benefactor, Syndra Silvane, revealed she was afflicted by the curse because she had been raised from the dead years ago. She consulted with contacts, including the Harpers, who traced the source of the curse to a necromantic artifact called the Soulmonger. The artifact is believed to be hidden within the ruins of an older civilization deep within Chult.
Syndra Silvane charged the adventurers with traveling to the peninsula of Chult to locate and neutralize the Soulmonger before the curse fully drained her life force. As a reward, she promised each character one rare and one uncommon magic item upon successful completion of the quest.
To expedite travel, Syndra Silvane intended to use a teleport spell to transport the party to the only major settlement in the region, Port Nyanzaru. The characters agreed, though Tidus Thalorien initially preferred to travel by sea.
Upon arrival, the party found themselves in the bustling, colorful, and noisy tropical city of Port Nyanzaru. Syndra Silvane departed to stay with her friend, Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu. She recommended the adventurers secure lodging at either the Thundering Lizard (for a "raucous time") or Kaya's House of Repose (for a "good night's sleep"), both located near the Red Bazaar.
Sir Caldryn Keyne resolved to seek lodging at the Thundering Lizard for revelry. Mistral Honeygleam spoke to the harbormaster (described as a large half gold dragon, half man) for directions to the Red Bazaar.While in the city, the party began investigating locations and seeking guidance:
Wakanga O'tamu: Sir Caldryn Keyne determined that the Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu dealt primarily in magic items and kept knowledge/information in scrolls.
Guide and Side Quest: While at the inn, a middle-aged woman approached the party, offering her services as a guide. She asked if they were heading toward Mbala. She revealed that she was seeking to eradicate the evil dwelling in the ghost town of Mbala, specifically the hag known as Nanny Pu'pu. The guide offered her services for free if the party agreed to help her destroy this evil. The party accepted this arrangement. The guide agreed to meet them at sunrise the following morning, confirming that she would handle guiding and navigation but the party was responsible for their food and water. The party purchased essential gear, including insect repellent balm and incense and rain catchers, and planned to start their expedition the next day.

item.title: Sir Caldryn Keyne
item.description:
Sir Caldryn Keyne is a wealthy and high-born member of the expedition, known for his polished demeanorDuring the initial phase in Port Nyanzaru, Sir Caldryn Keyne resolved to stay at the Thundering Lizard inn specifically for revelry. He actively gathered information, determining that Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu primarily deals in magic items
His activities led to an encounter with two guards who were seeking him regarding unauthorized attempts to recruit companions for his entourage. He successfully defused the situation by claiming he was merely seeking a young intern for tutelage, and was subsequently advised to seek an audience with Merchant Prince Jobal to follow proper protocol for hiring guides.
While resting in the jungle, Sir Caldryn Keyne engaged in "tutelage" of Scarloc Farmguard, sharing lessons on being a "chivalent hero" and serving wine from his private reserve.
In the subsequent dinosaur ambush, Sir Caldryn Keyne was immediately incapacitated by a powerful strike, requiring intervention from Mistral's familiar to save his life.

item.title: Port Nyanzaru
item.description:
Atmosphere and Appearance: The city is bustling, colorful, and noisy. Buildings are painted in bright shades of blue, green, orange, and salmon pink.Governance: The city is ruled by seven Merchant Princes, who control monopolies on all merchandise sold within the city.Economy and Hazards: The city is surrounded by enemies, including ferocious reptiles and murderous undead. Dinosaurs are commonly used as beasts of burden to haul carts and tow boats along canals. Ekene-Afa Ifan Talro'a Jessamine Jobal (Guides and Sellswords) Kwayothé Wakanga O'tamu (Magic and knowledge) Zhanthi The Red Bazaar: The Red Bazaar is where locals shop for everyday needs such as meat, vegetables, fruit, insect repellent, and rain catchers.
The Thundering Lizard (Inn): It is known that wilderness guides frequent this establishment looking for work.
Kaya's House of Repose (Inn): It is known to be much quieter than the Thundering Lizard.
The Grand Souk: Traders from up and down the Sword Coast come to buy timber, spices, medicines, dinosaur parts, ivory, and other riches of Chult here. The Lighthouse: A flame burns atop this structure day and night to guide ships through darkness, fog, and rain into the harbor Fort Nyanzaru: This fort contains a winch for raising and lowering an immense iron chain stretched between the fort and the lighthouse. When the chain is raised, it prevents any ship from sailing into or out of the harbor. The fort also mounts two ballistas. The Warehouse District: This area consists of warehouses used to store goods being shipped into or out of Port Nyanzaru. Canals are cut between the rows of warehouses, allowing ships to be towed directly to the desired warehouse by harnessed dinosaurs for easy loading and unloading.

item.title: Merchant Prince Jobal
item.description:
Jobal is one of the seven influential figures who hold the title of Merchant Prince and collectively rule the city of Port Nyanzaru.Jobal's specific domain relates to procuring manpower. Jobal deals exclusively in guides and sellswords. Like the other Merchant Princes, Jobal conducts business and governance from Goldenthrone
Jobal was introduced to the party through the local authorities while Sir Caldryn Keyne was attempting to recruit companions in Port Nyanzaru:
Sir Caldryn Keyne was intercepted by city guards and informed that attempts to recruit an entourage were unauthorized. The guards explained that Merchant Prince Jobal handles guides and sellswords in the city.
Sir Caldryn Keyne was advised to seek an audience with Jobal to follow proper protocol for hiring staff.

item.title: Chult
item.description:
Location and Composition: Chult is a peninsula ringed with mountains and choked with rainforests. It is described as a tropical wilderness. The interior consists mostly of jungles, plateaus, impassable mountains, and belching volcanoes. Climate: The region is consistently hot, humid, and rainy throughout the year. A day without rain is rare Navigation Challenges: Mapping the peninsula has historically been nearly impossible. Rivers are often sluggish, making it difficult to discern upstream from downstream. Perils of the Wilds: The interior jungle is prowled by enormous reptiles (dinosaurs) and an army of undead. The only major settlement and safest entry point into Chult is Port Nyanzaru, located on the coast at the south end of the Bay of Chult.The party is aware of various local groups and pervasive dangers: Undead: The jungle is overrun by a blight of undead creatures. Chultans: The native human population is known as Chultans.
Other Races: Members of every race can be found in Port Nyanzaru.

item.title: Soulmonger
item.description:
The Soulmonger is a necromantic artifact. It is the traced source of the worldwide "death curse".The function of the Soulmonger is directly tied to the two main aspects of the curse: Wasting Affliction: It is draining the life force from individuals who have been previously raised from the dead. For instance, the party's benefactor, Syndra Silvane. Preventing Resurrection: It is preventing future resurrections.
Intelligence Source: Information about the artifact was gathered by Syndra Silvane through her contacts, which included the Harpers. The Harpers determined the cause of the death curse is this device.
General Location: The Soulmonger is believed to be hidden on the peninsula of Chult.
Mission Goal: The characters have been charged with traveling to Chult to locate and neutralize the Soulmonger.

item.title: Mistral Honeygleam
item.description:
Upon arrival in Port Nyanzaru, Mistral secured directions to the Red Bazaar by speaking with the harbormaster. While staying at the inn, Mistral was observed spending time reading on the roof during a long rest.Before the expedition began, Mistral used their Druid abilities to produce Goodberry during breakfast, which helped provision the group for the journey.
During the first night camped in the jungle, Mistral worked with Tidus Thalorien and Scarloc Farmguard to secure the camp and prepare food. When the camp was violently ambushed by raptor-like dinosaurs, Mistral played a crucial role in saving Sir Caldryn Keyne's life after he was immediately incapacitated. Mistral achieved this by having their flying monkey familiar deliver a Goodberry, returning Sir Caldryn Keyne to 1 hit point.

item.title: Tidus Thalorien
item.description: During breakfast before the expedition, Tidus performed a ritual of prayer, giving thanks to the water goddess.As the party navigated the dense jungle terrain, Tidus aided Scarloc, the navigator, by using their daily Guidance cantrip. This magical assistance contributed to the party successfully charting a course and covering two hexes during their first day of travel.Tidus also assisted in securing the camp and preparing food during their first night camping in the jungle.

item.title: The Thundering Lizard
item.description:
The Thundering Lizard is a well-known establishment in Port Nyanzaru, recommended as a place of lodging for those seeking a lively atmosphere.It is characterized by its rowdy atmosphere. For adventurers seeking a stay focused on revelry,The Thundering Lizard played a role in the party's preparations before departing on their first expedition:
Sir Caldryn Keyne resolved to seek lodging at the Thundering Lizard specifically for revelry.
While staying at the inn, Sir Caldryn Keyne spent time there attempting to gather rumors from locals regarding the city's princes and the island of Chult.
This activity led to an encounter with two guards who were looking for Sir Caldryn Keyne.

item.title: Syndra Silvane
item.description:
The characters know the following about Syndra Silvane: Role and Status: She is the party's benefactor, a retired adventurer, and a merchant. The Death Curse: Syndra revealed that she is afflicted by the worldwide "death curse" because she was raised from the dead years ago. This affliction causes her to wither and die, a process that conventional clerics cannot stop. Past Experience: She mentioned that she had died once and been raised from the dead, and has since closed the door on her adventuring life. She also confirmed that she had already sent other, more experienced parties on this quest when pressed by the adventurers Syndra Silvane laid out the following information regarding the expedition: The Source of the Curse: Her contacts, including the Harpers, determined that the death curse originates from a necromantic artifact called the Soulmonger.
The Objective: She tasked the adventurers with traveling to the peninsula of Chult to locate and neutralize the Soulmonger before the curse claims her life. Reward: She promised each character one rare and one uncommon magic item upon the successful completion of the quest.
Travel and Support: To expedite their journey, Syndra used a teleport spell to transport the group directly to Port Nyanzaru, the only major settlement in Chult. She provided the party with an incomplete map of Chult.
Post-Arrival Plans: Upon arrival, Syndra departed to stay with her friend, Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu. She recommended securing lodging at either the Thundering Lizard or Kaya's House of Repose. She informed the characters that Wakanga O'tamu is one of the seven merchant princes who rule the city.

item.title: DID YOU LEVEL UP LAST SESSION
item.guid: DID YOU LEVEL UP LAST SESSION.md

item.title: Introduction
item.description: Campaign Overview A death curse plaguing all of Toril emanates from the jungle island of Chult. Ancient ruins and hordes of undead fill the mostly unexplored and dangerous jungles. The mercantile city of Port Nyanzaru stands as a bastion of civilization on the northern edge of the jungle. The tone of the game is fantasy Indiana Jones / The Mummy. Overall the jungle and especially the tomb are very dangerous and unforgiving; though some encounters will be light and even slightly 'wacky' fun. The jungles of Chult are largely unexplored and very dangerous, filled with dinosaurs, strange goblins, and a lot of undead. Wilderness survival is a key element of much of the game. The peninsula's only city is Port Nyanzaru, a bustling port built on trade, filled with unique wonders, and run by powerful and enigmatic Merchant Princes. Character Creation: The Death Curse (in part) affects people who have been previously raised from the dead (They started wasting away). If you like, your character can have folks in their life who have been raised from the dead before the adventure starts; this might help motivate them to pursue the main external goal of the story (but that's not required!). The Death Curse started about 20 days before the start of the adventure and is widely talked about. Characters can be from Chult, from the Sword Coast, or from anywhere. The current start idea is that you are all adventurer’s with a benefactor in Baldur’s Gate (I might change this based on your characters) The game will probably begin in Baldur's Gate unless all / most of you decide your characters are from Chult. Native Races from Chult are: Humans, Dwarves, Tabaxi and Aarakocra (Let me know if you want more details) The Campaign is supposed to be difficult, so be as Meta in your character creation as you want, feel free to work together to synergize characters and mechanics All Dnd 5.5e content published by WOTC is fair game (if it was reprinted for 2024, you have to use the new stuff 😉). Any 3rd party / Homebrew just run it by me. Characters start at level 2, and get the basic starting equipment or the gold equivalent. You may use point buy for your stats, or roll for your stats (4d6dl 6 times to create an array.) Mechanics to Keep in Mind: Revive magic doesn't work anymore. Traveling On the map of Chult, each hex measures 10 miles across. Characters moving at a normal pace can travel 1 hex per day on foot through coastal, jungle, mountain, swamp, or wasteland terrain. They can travel 2 hexes per day if they're traveling by canoe on a river or lake. If characters move at a fast pace, they have a 50% chance to advance 1 additional hex that day. Characters moving at a fast pace take a penalty to their passive Wisdom (Perception) scores. If characters set a slow pace, they have a 50% chance to advance 1 fewer hex that day. Characters moving at a slow pace can move stealthily. As long as they're not in the open, they can try to surprise or sneak by other creatures they encounter. Dehydration At the end of each day, any character who hasn't drunk at least 2 gallons of fresh water must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or suffer 1 level of exhaustion. The saving throw is made with disadvantage if the character is wearing medium armor, heavy armor, or heavy clothing. Characters traveling at a fast pace, instead of a normal or slow pace, take a penalty on their saving throws against dehydration. A long rest will not restore exhaustion until the character consumes enough fresh water. Weather Chult is hot, humid, and rainy throughout the year. The temperature regularly climbs as high as 35 degrees C during the day and seldom falls below 20 degrees C even at night. A day without rain is rare, but rain varies from a steady mist to drenching downpours. Visibility in heavy rain is limited to 50 yards. Beyond that distance, only Huge or larger objects can be distinguished. Missile weapon ranges are halved during rain.
